researched_category.notes: The YouTube Data API v3 is Google's official API for integrating YouTube functionality into applications. Its caption endpoints specifically provide programmatic access to video transcripts/subtitles.

one_liner.value: Google's official YouTube Data API v3 that provides programmatic access to YouTube captions/transcripts among other video platform features.

methods.notes: API key works for public data endpoints (search, videos, channels). OAuth 2.0 is required for captions.list and captions.download — these need the youtube.force-ssl or youtubepartner scope. API key alone cannot access caption tracks.

access.notes: Any developer with a Google account can create a Google Cloud project, enable the YouTube Data API v3, and obtain credentials. The free tier provides 10,000 quota units/day. No credit card required. For higher quota, a formal review is needed. OAuth consent screen verification may be needed for sensitive scopes. Captions download requires the user to have permission to edit the video, which means OAuth as the video owner or content manager.

summary.value: Fully documented public REST API covering videos, channels, playlists, captions, comments, search, and more. The captions sub-API supports list, insert, update, download, and delete operations. Captions.list returns metadata only; captions.download fetches actual transcript content in SRT, VTT, SBV, SCC, or TTML formats. Quota costs: captions.list = 50 units, captions.download = 200 units. Requires OAuth 2.0 (youtube.force-ssl scope) for caption operations.

agent_interface.notes: Multiple community MCP servers exist for YouTube. The youtube-data-mcp-server (github.com/icraft2170/youtube-data-mcp-server) provides getTranscripts tool using YouTube Data API. Another community MCP (github.com/ZubeidHendricks/youtube-mcp-server) offers transcripts_getTranscript. The open-source youtube-transcript-api Python library (jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api) is widely used and doesn't require API keys. Composio has a YouTube toolkit (composio.dev/toolkits/youtube) but no specific 'YouTube Transcript' app.

rationale.value: The official YouTube Data API v3 does have public endpoints for captions (list/download), but they strictly require OAuth 2.0 authentication with the youtube.force-ssl scope — an API key alone is insufficient. Furthermore, captions.download requires the authenticated user to have edit permissions on the video, making it unsuitable for fetching transcripts of arbitrary public videos. The quota system (10,000 units/day free) is also restrictive for transcript-heavy use cases (200 units per download). However, alternative approaches exist: the open-source youtube-transcript-api library (7.8k stars) fetches transcripts without OAuth by scraping YouTube's internal timedtext endpoint. For a pure agent toolkit using only Google's official API, this is gated by OAuth + edit-permission requirements. Using the community library is buildable today but uses an undocumented/scraping approach.

s1.quote: You need a Google Account to access the Google API Console, request an API key, and register your application.
s1.title: YouTube Data API Overview

s2.quote: With the YouTube Data API, you can add a variety of YouTube features to your application. Use the API to upload videos, manage playlists and subscriptions, update channel settings, and more.
s2.title: YouTube Data API

s3.quote: The YouTube Data API supports the OAuth 2.0 protocol for authorizing access to private user data.
s3.title: Implementing OAuth 2.0 Authorization

s4.quote: This request requires authorization with at least one of the following scopes: https://www.googleapis.com/auth/youtube.force-ssl, https://www.googleapis.com/auth/youtubepartner
s4.title: Captions: list

s5.quote: Yes — getting a YouTube API key through Google Cloud Console is free, and the YouTube Data API v3 provides a free tier of 10,000 quota units per day.
s5.title: YouTube API Key Guide

s6.quote: The YouTube Data API v3 has no per-call monetary cost. The constraint is the quota system: 10,000 units per project per day by default.
s6.title: YouTube API Pricing Guide

s7.quote: This method is requires the user to have permission to edit the video. Quota impact: A call to this method has a quota cost of 200 units.
s7.title: Captions: download

s8.quote: A caption resource represents a YouTube caption track. A caption track is associated with exactly one YouTube video.
s8.title: Captions resource

s9.quote: A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server implementation utilizing the YouTube Data API. It allows AI language models to interact with YouTube content through a standardized interface.
s9.title: youtube-data-mcp-server

s10.quote: A Model Context Protocol server that enables retrieval of transcripts from YouTube videos.
s10.title: MCP YouTube Transcript Server

s11.quote: This is a python API which allows you to get the transcript/subtitles for a given YouTube video. It also works for automatically generated subtitles and it does not require an API key nor a headless browser.
s11.title: jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api

s12.quote: It does not provide open access to auto-generated captions for arbitrary public videos. Caption download flows require more than a simple API key.
s12.title: Best YouTube Transcript APIs Compared

limitations: The term 'YouTube Transcript' is ambiguous — it could refer to the official YouTube Data API v3 caption endpoints, the open-source youtube-transcript-api library, or third-party hosted transcript APIs. The user provided the YouTube Data API v3 URL, so that is the primary focus.
The official captions.download endpoint requires OAuth with edit permission on the video, which is the core blocker for arbitrary transcript retrieval.
Several community MCP servers exist but none are officially published by Google.
